pattern: Tracking the lowest price seen so far lets each day's best possible profit be read off in one pass, turning a pairwise search into linear time.
hints: You must buy before you sell, so for each selling day only earlier prices matter.
If you remember the cheapest price encountered up to today, today's best profit is just today's price minus that minimum.
Sweep left to right keeping the running minimum price and the best profit so far; update both at each step and never let profit go negative.

edge_inputs.description: buy low then sell at a later peak
strictly decreasing prices give zero profit
the best buy comes after an early dip but the peak is earlier
two equal prices give no gain
min then max prices span the value range

stress.description: long random walk of prices within bounds
